name: Flyout Tours
summary: A travel-focused product platform involving frontend, backend, and database-driven workflows.
description: Flyout Tours represents Gufran’s work on a product-oriented application rather than just a college project. It involved handling application workflows, product-related data, and real engineering tasks around integration and deployment.
problem: Travel and booking-oriented products require structured handling of packages, product information, user-facing flows, and reliable data updates.
solution: Worked on building and improving product workflows, frontend/backend integration, and database-connected application features.
responsibilities: Worked on frontend and backend integration tasks.
Handled product-related implementation and data updates.
Contributed to real-world product workflows rather than only demo features.
whatItShows: Experience working on a live product setup
Comfort with production debugging and iteration

name: Whirlpool
summary: A collaborative coding environment with real-time interaction features.
description: Whirlpool is a real-time collaborative code editor concept focused on shared editing and synchronized interactions.
problem: Collaborative coding tools need low-latency communication, synchronized editing, and a clean interface for multi-user interaction.
solution: Built a collaborative editor experience using frontend and real-time communication tools.
responsibilities: Integrated real-time communication for synchronized usage.

name: Agent Ascend
summary: A React-based component or UI system focused on reusable building blocks.
description: Agent Ascend appears to be a frontend-focused project around reusable components and UI architecture.
problem: Frontend projects benefit from reusable components, consistency, and maintainable design systems.
solution: Built reusable frontend components and structured UI patterns using React and TypeScript.
responsibilities: Worked with typed frontend development patterns.

name: Event Manager
summary: An event/task management style application with modern web tooling.
description: Event Manager is a full-stack style application demonstrating modern app development patterns and integration workflows.
problem: Event and task workflows require structured data, good UX, and reliable state handling.
solution: Built a modern app using a React-based framework and backend-connected data workflows.
responsibilities: Handled feature integration and app workflows.
whatItShows: Ability to combine frontend, backend, and AI integration
Comfort with modern web application tooling

name: College FAQ Chatbot
summary: A college-focused FAQ chatbot built with Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, and retrieval-based AI workflows.
description: This project is one of the strongest representations of Gufran’s interest in AI-integrated backend applications. It combines document understanding, retrieval, embeddings, and LLM-based response generation in a practical student-facing use case.
problem: Students often struggle to find accurate answers about college-related processes, documents, and information spread across multiple sources.
solution: Built a retrieval-based chatbot that stores and retrieves relevant context before generating answers, using backend services and AI model integrations.
responsibilities: Designed backend services and data flow for chatbot interactions.
Worked on retrieval pipelines, embeddings, and vector-store integration.
Integrated local AI/LLM workflows using Ollama-based tooling.
Handled PostgreSQL and supporting service setup.
whatItShows: Strong interest in AI-enabled backend systems
Ability to combine Spring Boot, databases, Docker, and LLM tooling

name: E-Commerce
summary: An e-commerce application demonstrating backend workflows, data management, and business-style application structure.
description: The E-Commerce project reflects work on a business-style application with product-oriented backend logic and database workflows.
problem: E-commerce systems need structured handling of products, user actions, and database-backed workflows.
solution: Built a backend-connected application with support for core commerce flows and data management.
responsibilities: Worked on backend logic and application structure.
Applied Spring Boot to a business-style application use case.
whatItShows: Comfort with Spring Boot and relational databases
Ability to work on product-style data flows
